mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element into a binary heap-based priority queue?
mcqs.explanation: In a binary heap, insertion places the element at the end and then performs a sift-up (bubble-up) operation along the path to the root, which takes O(log n) time proportional to the heap height.

mcqs.question: In a max-heap, which element is always at the root?
mcqs.explanation: A max-heap maintains the heap property where every parent node is greater than or equal to its children. Therefore, the maximum element is always at the root.

mcqs.question: What distinguishes a double-ended priority queue (DEPQ) from a single-ended priority queue?

options.text: A DEPQ supports both insert and delete operations
A DEPQ allows efficient access to both the minimum and maximum elements
A DEPQ uses two separate arrays instead of one
A DEPQ only works with integer keys

mcqs.explanation: A double-ended priority queue supports efficient retrieval and deletion of both the minimum and maximum priority elements, unlike a single-ended PQ which supports only one extreme.

mcqs.question: Which data structure can be used to implement a double-ended priority queue?
mcqs.explanation: A min-max heap is a specialized heap where levels alternate between min-levels and max-levels, allowing O(1) access to both the minimum (root) and maximum elements, with O(log n) insertions and deletions.

mcqs.question: During a delete-max operation on a max-heap of n elements, what is the process?

options.text: Remove the last element and rebuild the entire heap
Remove the root, replace it with the last element, and sift down
Search for the max element linearly and remove it
Remove the root and shift all elements up by one position

mcqs.explanation: Delete-max removes the root (maximum element), replaces it with the last element in the heap, and then performs a sift-down (heapify) operation to restore the heap property in O(log n) time.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of finding the minimum element in a min-heap?
mcqs.explanation: In a min-heap, the minimum element is always at the root, so finding it takes constant time O(1).

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'percolate up' operation in a binary heap?

options.text: To restore the heap property after insertion

mcqs.explanation: The 'percolate up' operation is used to restore the heap property after a new element is inserted, by comparing the new element with its parent and swapping them if necessary.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a double-ended priority queue?

options.text: It only supports insertion and deletion of the minimum element
It only supports insertion and deletion of the maximum element
It supports insertion and deletion of both the minimum and maximum elements
It does not support insertion or deletion of any elements

mcqs.explanation: A double-ended priority queue supports efficient insertion and deletion of both the minimum and maximum elements.
